registry.description: Self-control is the inner capacity to govern oneself — to choose what one does with one's appetites, impulses, and reactions rather than being governed by them. The Greek vocabulary here (egkrateia, sōphrosynē) covers the ability to hold back, the soundness of mind that keeps the whole person in good order. Self-control is listed as a fruit of the Spirit: it is not primarily the product of effort but of the Spirit's work reshaping the inner person. Without it, strength, intelligence, and desire all become dangerous rather than good.

terms.lsj_entry:
σώφρων, 
Epic dialect and poetry σᾰόφρων (as in [Refs 8th c.BC+], ονος, ὁ, ἡ: neuter σῶφρον:—properly of sound mind (from σῶς, φρήν, [Refs 5th c.BC+]: hence, discreet, prudent, οὐκ ἄν με σαόφρονα μυθήσαιο ἔμμεναι [Refs 8th c.BC+]; opposed to ἄφρων, [Refs 6th c.BC+]; opposed to νήπιος, [Refs]; opposed to ἀνόητος, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__2 of things, τοῖσι λόγοις σῶφρον ἔπεστιν ἄνθος [Refs 5th c.BC+]; σ. οἶκτος reasonable compassion, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; σῶφρόν ἐστι with infinitive, [Refs]
__II in Attic dialect, especially having control over the sensual desires, temperate, self-controlled, chaste (σώφρων ὁ μετρίας ἐπιθυμίας ἔχων [Refs 5th c.BC+]; γυνὴ σ. [NT+5th c.BC+]
__II.2 of things, σ. γνώμη [Refs 4th c.BC+]; σ. ὑμέναιοι, λέχη, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; τράπεζα, δίαιτα, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ἀριστοκρατία moderate, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; χάρις[Refs 5th c.BC+]
__II.3 τὸ σῶφρον, ={σωφροσύνη}, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; σοῦ τὸ σ. [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__III adverb -όνως[Refs 5th c.BC+]; δικαίως πράττοντες καὶ σ. [Refs]; σ. ἐφέπεσθαι cautiously, [Refs 5th c.BC+] adopt a more moderate constitution, [Refs 5th c.BC+] but -εστέρως [Refs 5th c.BC+]: superlative -έστατα[Refs 5th c.BC+]

verses.esv_text: Ecc 3:9 What gain has the worker from his toil ?
verses.preview_html: Ecc 3:9 What gain has the worker from his toil?

verses.esv_text: Ecc 4:1 Again I saw all the oppressions that are done under the sun . And behold , the tears of the oppressed , and they had no one to comfort them! On the side of their oppressors there was power , and there was no one to comfort them.
verses.preview_html: Ecc 4:1 Again I saw all the oppressions that are done under the sun. And behold, the tears of the oppressed, and they had no one to comfort them! On the side of their oppressors there was power, and there was no one to comfort them.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 4:13 Better was a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king who no longer knew how to take advice .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 4:13 Better was a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king who no longer knew how to take advice.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 4:15 I saw all the living who move about under the sun , along with that youth who was to stand in the king’s place.
verses.preview_html: Ecc 4:15 I saw all the living who move about under the sun, along with that youth who was to stand in the king’s place.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 4:16 There was no end of all the people , all of whom he led . Yet those who come later will not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and a striving after wind .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 4:16 There was no end of all the people, all of whom he led. Yet those who come later will not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and a striving after wind.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 4:2 And I thought the dead who are already dead more fortunate than the living who are still alive .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 4:2 And I thought the dead who are already dead more fortunate than the living who are still alive.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 4:3 But better than both is he who has not yet been and has not seen the evil deeds that are done under the sun .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 4:3 But better than both is he who has not yet been and has not seen the evil deeds that are done under the sun.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 4:9 Two are better than one , because they have a good reward for their toil .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 4:9 Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their toil.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 5:1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God . To draw near to listen is better than to offer the sacrifice of fools , for they do not know that they are doing evil .

verses.esv_text: Ecc 5:15 As he came from his mother’s womb he shall go again , naked as he came , and shall take nothing for his toil that he may carry away in his hand .

verses.esv_text: Ecc 5:18 Behold , what I have seen to be good and fitting is to eat and drink and find enjoyment in all the toil with which one toils under the sun the few days of his life that God has given him, for this is his lot .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 5:18 Behold, what I have seen to be good and fitting is to eat and drink and find enjoyment in all the toil with which one toils under the sun the few days of his life that God has given him, for this is his lot.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 5:19 Everyone also to whom God has given wealth and possessions and power to enjoy them , and to accept his lot and rejoice in his toil — this is the gift of God .

verses.esv_text: Ecc 5:4 When you vow a vow to God , do not delay paying it, for he has no pleasure in fools . Pay what you vow .

verses.esv_text: Ecc 5:5 It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 5:5 It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 6:1 There is an evil that I have seen under the sun , and it lies heavy on mankind :

verses.esv_text: Ecc 6:10 Whatever has come to be has already been named , and it is known what man is, and that he is not able to dispute with one stronger than he .

verses.esv_text: Ecc 6:12 For who knows what is good for man while he lives the few days of his vain life , which he passes like a shadow ? For who can tell man what will be after him under the sun ?

verses.esv_text: Ecc 6:2 a man to whom God gives wealth , possessions , and honor , so that he lacks nothing of all that he desires , yet God does not give him power to enjoy them, but a stranger enjoys them. This is vanity ; it is a grievous evil .

verses.preview_html: Ecc 4:15 I saw all the living who move about under the sun, along with that youth who was to stand in the king’s place.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 4:16 There was no end of all the people , all of whom he led . Yet those who come later will not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and a striving after wind .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 4:16 There was no end of all the people, all of whom he led. Yet those who come later will not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and a striving after wind.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 4:2 And I thought the dead who are already dead more fortunate than the living who are still alive .

verses.esv_text: Ecc 4:3 But better than both is he who has not yet been and has not seen the evil deeds that are done under the sun .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 4:3 But better than both is he who has not yet been and has not seen the evil deeds that are done under the sun.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 4:9 Two are better than one , because they have a good reward for their toil .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 4:9 Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their toil.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 5:1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God . To draw near to listen is better than to offer the sacrifice of fools , for they do not know that they are doing evil .

verses.esv_text: Ecc 5:18 Behold , what I have seen to be good and fitting is to eat and drink and find enjoyment in all the toil with which one toils under the sun the few days of his life that God has given him, for this is his lot .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 5:18 Behold, what I have seen to be good and fitting is to eat and drink and find enjoyment in all the toil with which one toils under the sun the few days of his life that God has given him, for this is his lot.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 5:19 Everyone also to whom God has given wealth and possessions and power to enjoy them , and to accept his lot and rejoice in his toil — this is the gift of God .

verses.esv_text: Ecc 5:4 When you vow a vow to God , do not delay paying it, for he has no pleasure in fools . Pay what you vow .

verses.esv_text: Ecc 5:5 It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay .
verses.preview_html: Ecc 5:5 It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay.

verses.esv_text: Ecc 6:1 There is an evil that I have seen under the sun , and it lies heavy on mankind :

verses.esv_text: Ecc 6:10 Whatever has come to be has already been named , and it is known what man is, and that he is not able to dispute with one stronger than he .

verses.esv_text: Ecc 6:12 For who knows what is good for man while he lives the few days of his vain life , which he passes like a shadow ? For who can tell man what will be after him under the sun ?

verses.esv_text: Ecc 6:2 a man to whom God gives wealth , possessions , and honor , so that he lacks nothing of all that he desires , yet God does not give him power to enjoy them, but a stranger enjoys them. This is vanity ; it is a grievous evil .

verses.preview_html: Neh 2:18 And I told them of the hand of my God that had been upon me for good, and also of the words that the king had spoken to me. And they said, “Let us rise up and build.” So they strengthened their hands for the good work.

verses.esv_text: Neh 2:19 But when Sanballat the Horonite and Tobiah the Ammonite servant and Geshem the Arab heard of it, they jeered at us and despised us and said , “ What is this thing that you are doing ? Are you rebelling against the king ?”

verses.esv_text: Ecc 2:12 So I turned to consider wisdom and madness and folly . For what can the man do who comes after the king ? Only what has already been done .

verses.esv_text: Ecc 2:3 I searched with my heart how to cheer my body with wine —my heart still guiding me with wisdom —and how to lay hold on folly , till I might see what was good for the children of man to do under heaven during the few days of their life .

verses.esv_text: Num 9:18 At the command of the Lord the people of Israel set out , and at the command of the Lord they camped . As long as the cloud rested over the tabernacle , they remained in camp .

verses.esv_text: Num 9:20 Sometimes the cloud was a few days over the tabernacle , and according to the command of the Lord they remained in camp ; then according to the command of the Lord they set out .

verses.esv_text: Num 9:21 And sometimes the cloud remained from evening until morning . And when the cloud lifted in the morning , they set out , or if it continued for a day and a night , when the cloud lifted they set out .
